description.bannermod.recruit:
The Recruit is a important warrior who relies only on melee weapons.
Swift on his feet and able to carry a shield, he can adapt to many situations.
Though simple in training, he is reliable in almost every kind of fight.
description.bannermod.shieldman:
The Shieldman is a steadfast defender.
Armed only with melee weapons, he excels with the shield, blocking attacks with shorter recovery time.
Though his steps are slower, his endurance and health make him well-suited for long, grinding battles.
description.bannermod.bowman:
The Bowman is a fast and accurate archer, striking enemies from a distance with precision shots.
When needed, he can draw a sword to defend himself in close combat.
However, he cannot carry a shield and has no skill with the crossbow.
description.bannermod.crossbowman:
The Crossbowman is a disciplined ranged fighter, delivering powerful and precise bolts even at long range.
His shots are fast, and they hit hard and true.
If needed, he can wield a sword in close quarters, but he cannot carry shields or use bows.
description.bannermod.horseman:
The Horseman is a mounted warrior who charges fearlessly into enemy lines.
In free wander mode, his horse charge scatters and knocks back enemies, breaking their ranks.
He can fight with melee weapons and shields, making him both strong in defense and devastating in attack.
description.bannermod.nomad:
The Nomad is a skilled rider and master of the bow.
In free wander mode, he circles his enemies on horseback, loosing arrows while avoiding their attacks.
Agile and elusive, he fights with the bow and sword, striking swiftly before retreating again into motion.
description.bannermod.scout:
The Scout is a fast and watchful companion.
He can spot enemies when they come into view and give clear information about their numbers and strength.
In battle, he can fight with a bow from range or with a sword in close combat.
description.bannermod.messenger:
The Messenger is a companion of peace.
He can send messages or treaties to faraway players or factions and, if needed, carry a gift in his hand.
Though skilled with sword and bow, he cannot be harmed while on a mission, for his duty to protect diplomacy.
description.bannermod.captain:
The Captain is master of the ship.
He can sail to chosen places and take command of the cannons on board.
His shots are precise against enemy ships and can also strike targets on land.
In close battle, he fights well with sword and shield.
description.bannermod.commander:
The Commander is a strong leader.
He can patrol chosen positions and guide a group of recruits, leading them from point to point.
With him at the front, his men move with order and strength.
gui.bannermod.map.claim_state.empty: No claims on the synced map.
description.bannermod.peaceTreaty: When accepting this treaty both factions cannot attack each other for the duration.
chat.bannermod.text.messenger_target_pla: %s: I arrived, but %s did not accept our treaty proposal.

gui.bannermod.workers.command.tooltip.re: Stops selected workers, clears their active assignment, and gives control back.
description.bannermod.workers.farmer: Plants crops using seeds from nearby storage and harvests fields when they are ready. Automatically works on farmland within range and keeps crops growing efficiently.
description.bannermod.workers.miner: Mines a defined area and places torches while working. Provides safety by filling holes with cobblestone and can optionally cover the ground to prevent dangerous falls.
description.bannermod.workers.lumberjack: Cuts down trees inside a lumber area and can strip logs or replant saplings when instructed. Keeps forests managed and renewable.
description.bannermod.workers.builder: Builds saved structures block by block and requires all needed materials. Collects resources from the nearest depot before starting construction.
description.bannermod.workers.merchant: Trades with other players on your behalf using predefined trade offers. Can take required items from a dedicated depot if permitted.
description.bannermod.workers.fisherman: Uses a fishing rod to fish in a defined fishing area and casts lines along a set route. Collects nearby fish and items, stores them in a nearby depot, and handles the rod carefully to extend its durability.
description.bannermod.workers.animalFarm: Takes care of animals in defined pens. Breeds the animals faster and efficient than a player could .

gui.recruits.command.tooltip.tight: Toggle tight formation spacing (50% closer together).
gui.recruits.command.tooltip.hold_fire: Disable the use of Ranged weapons.
gui.recruits.command.tooltip.fire_at_wil: Enable the use of Ranged weapons.
gui.recruits.command.tooltip.stance_loos: Allow the formation to chase more freely and keep wider spacing.
gui.recruits.command.tooltip.stance_line: Keep the line tighter and limit how far troops leave their formation slot.
gui.recruits.command.tooltip.stance_shie: Hold the tightest leash for shield-wall style fighting and cohesion.
gui.recruits.command.tooltip.hold_strate: Stop the ongoing strategic fire command.
gui.recruits.command.tooltip.passive: Make the recruits not attacking at all.
gui.recruits.command.tooltip.neutral: Make the recruits attack only hostile living.
gui.recruits.command.tooltip.aggressive: Make the recruits attack all enemy players and recruits on sight.
gui.recruits.command.tooltip.raid: Make the recruits attack all living on sight.

description.recruits.recruit:
The Recruit is a important warrior who relies only on melee weapons.
Swift on his feet and able to carry a shield, he can adapt to many situations.
Though simple in training, he is reliable in almost every kind of fight.
description.recruits.shieldman:
The Shieldman is a steadfast defender.
Armed only with melee weapons, he excels with the shield, blocking attacks with shorter recovery time.
Though his steps are slower, his endurance and health make him well-suited for long, grinding battles.
description.recruits.bowman:
The Bowman is a fast and accurate archer, striking enemies from a distance with precision shots.
When needed, he can draw a sword to defend himself in close combat.
However, he cannot carry a shield and has no skill with the crossbow.
description.recruits.crossbowman:
The Crossbowman is a disciplined ranged fighter, delivering powerful and precise bolts even at long range.
His shots are fast, and they hit hard and true.
If needed, he can wield a sword in close quarters, but he cannot carry shields or use bows.
description.recruits.horseman:
The Horseman is a mounted warrior who charges fearlessly into enemy lines.
In free wander mode, his horse charge scatters and knocks back enemies, breaking their ranks.
He can fight with melee weapons and shields, making him both strong in defense and devastating in attack.
description.recruits.nomad:
The Nomad is a skilled rider and master of the bow.
In free wander mode, he circles his enemies on horseback, loosing arrows while avoiding their attacks.
Agile and elusive, he fights with the bow and sword, striking swiftly before retreating again into motion.
description.recruits.scout:
The Scout is a fast and watchful companion.
He can spot enemies when they come into view and give clear information about their numbers and strength.
In battle, he can fight with a bow from range or with a sword in close combat.
description.recruits.messenger:
The Messenger is a companion of peace.
He can send messages or treaties to faraway players or factions and, if needed, carry a gift in his hand.
Though skilled with sword and bow, he cannot be harmed while on a mission, for his duty to protect diplomacy.
description.recruits.captain:
The Captain is master of the ship.
He can sail to chosen places and take command of the cannons on board.
His shots are precise against enemy ships and can also strike targets on land.
In close battle, he fights well with sword and shield.
description.recruits.commander:
The Commander is a strong leader.
He can patrol chosen positions and guide a group of recruits, leading them from point to point.
With him at the front, his men move with order and strength.

item.bannermod.banner_almanac.tooltip.sc: Covers settlement, workers, recruits, politics, war, and sieges.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_1:
BannerMod Almanac

Three layers rule the mod:
1. Claim = protected Overworld land.
2. Settlement = your live base.
3. State = your political side.

Hotkeys:
M map and claims
U War Room
R recruits
X workers
V prefab preview.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_2:
First 10 minutes

1. Claim an Overworld chunk.
2. Create a state in War Room -> States -> Create or use /bannermod state create <name>.
3. Craft Settlement Surveyor Tool and Building Placement Wand.
4. Build a Starter Fort.
5. Open the survey board, set anchor, mark AUTHORITY_POINT, then capture one INTERIOR zone for the whole usable fort, courtyard, and side/back wings before you press Validate. Split fort interior zones are not supported yet.
6. Global blockers: missing required zone, anchor outside every selected zone, bad zone volume, or no settlement for non-fort modes.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_3:
Surveyor controls

Right-click air: open board.
Shift-right-click block: reopen board.
Normal right-click block: if no anchor, set anchor; otherwise save corner A, then corner B for the selected role.

Color key
- gold/wood = fort walls and towers
- orange = authority anchor
- blue = usable interior/courtyard
- brown = wings, storage, or barracks space

Global rules
- Need at least 1 zone
- Zone volume must be >0 and <=262144
- Required roles depend on mode
- Anchor must be inside at least 1 selected zone
- INTERIOR, SLEEPING, and WORK_ZONE cannot overlap the same primary roles of a different building in the same settlement
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_4:
INTERIOR and homes

INTERIOR is the usable air volume, not just walls. A walkable cell needs air at feet, air at head, and solid floor below. Roof coverage checks for any roof block 2-8 blocks above those walkable cells. For Starter Fort, INTERIOR should be one zone covering the whole usable fort, courtyard, and side/back wings; split interior zones are not supported yet.

Starter Fort:
- required: AUTHORITY_POINT + INTERIOR
- warnings: authority zone misses anchor, no banner near anchor, <64 walkable cells, <80% roof, unclear entrance
- success: bootstrap settlement

House:
- required: INTERIOR + SLEEPING
- blockers: <8 walkable cells, <70% roof, no bed
- bed fallback: in/near SLEEPING, in/near INTERIOR, or within 12 blocks of anchor
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_5:
Work-zone validators

Farm:
- WORK_ZONE
- anchor within 24 blocks
- >=24 farmland or crop spots
- capacity scales by 48 fields

Mine:
- WORK_ZONE
- anchor within 32 blocks
- >=24 stone/deepslate/cobble/ore face blocks
- warning if anchor sees sky
- capacity scales by 64 face blocks

Lumber Camp:
- WORK_ZONE
- anchor within 32 blocks
- productivity >=12 where productivity = logs + saplings/2
- capacity scales by productivity/12
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_6:
Specialized validators

Smithy:
- INTERIOR + WORK_ZONE
- >=70% roof
- >=1 anvil
- >=1 furnace or blast furnace
- one anvil-furnace pair within 4 blocks

Storage:
- STORAGE zone
- >=1 chest or barrel

Architect Workshop:
- INTERIOR + WORK_ZONE
- >=16 walkable cells
- >=70% roof
- >=1 crafting table

Inspect Existing reads type, state, capacity, quality, and vacancy hint at a validated anchor.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_7:
After successful founding

A valid Starter Fort creates or binds the claim, creates the settlement, spawns starter workers, and gives free citizens for vacancies. Eligible existing claims that auto-bootstrap through worker growth use the same model.

Ready immediately:
farmer

Waiting for player setup:
miner -> mine
lumberjack -> lumber camp
builder -> architect workshop or build area

Free citizens convert only after they walk to an open building anchor.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_8:
Building Wand and market

Wand modes:
PLACE = place prefab
VALIDATE = score a prefab-style build
REGISTER = save or register a player-built area

Surveyor validator modes are: Starter Fort, House, Farm, Mine, Lumber Camp, Smithy, Storage, Architect Workshop, and Inspect Existing.
Market is not a surveyor validator mode. Register market areas with the wand.

Starter order:
Fort -> Storage -> Farm -> Homes -> Market -> Mine/Lumber/Architect
Promotion still needs Fort or Town Hall + Storage + Market.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_9:
Citizens and workers

Citizens are free population. They fill vacancies and convert near the assigned anchor.

Common worker jobs:
farmer, miner, lumberjack, builder, merchant, fisherman, animal farmer.

If workers do nothing, check:
claim ownership
worker side
registered building
open vacancy near the citizen
available storage and goods
territory not captured or degraded.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_10:
Logistics and economy

Storage shows stockpile capacity and route state.
Market is the economy hub.
The governor logistics panel is your main ledger: shortages, work orders, stockpile, missing goods, strategic role, route cost, sea-route state, and project hints.

Ship-backed routes show loading, travelling, unloading, completed, needs ship, or blocked cargo.
One route is fragile.
Water access is cheap supply.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_11:
Governor flow

Promote an owned recruit from its inventory.
Governor needs:
recruit level 7+
owner present
friendly claimed settlement link

Governor screen shows:
citizens
taxes
incidents
recommendations
treasury
logistics

Policy sliders:
Garrison Priority
Fortification Priority
Tax Pressure.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_12:
Recruits and command

Use R for army command.
Main orders:
Hold
Follow
Regroup
Wander
Come to me
Patrol
Move to position
Face
Attack
Ranged fire
Aggro
Siege-machine crew orders

Keep food and payment with the army owner when upkeep matters.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_13:
Combat doctrine

Stances:
LOOSE = flexible
LINE_HOLD = stable front
SHIELD_WALL = strongest frontal defense

Important rules:
shields work mostly forward
pikes and spears reach farther
second rank can attack through allies
side hits are stronger
back hits are strongest
shield wall is slower but much tougher.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_14:
War Room and politics

Press U. Use it for:
States screen
war list
state details
government form
ally invites
siege placement
battle-window banner

Allies are consent-based: invite, then accept or decline.
Republic lets co-leaders share more political authority.
Monarchy keeps key authority on the leader.
item.bannermod.banner_almanac.page_15:
Wars, sieges, revolts, and limits

Use War Room or commands to declare war.
Place siege standards where economy matters: bridges, stockpiles, markets, ports, route junctions.
Revolts exist but objective-based revolt flow is still incomplete.
Small Ships sea trade is visible through settlement logistics when a compatible carrier is bound.
If something is denied, usually check leader rights, battle window, side ownership, claim ownership, or missing infrastructure.
bannermod.surveyor.tooltip.use: Right-click air: open the survey board. Shift-right-click any block: open it while aiming at your build.
bannermod.surveyor.tooltip.shift: Normal right-click on blocks: set anchor first, then capture two corners for the selected role.
bannermod.surveyor.tooltip.manual_only: Surveyor holograms are guide-only. They never place blocks for you.
bannermod.surveyor.tooltip.fort_rules: Starter Fort founding needs AUTHORITY_POINT plus one INTERIOR zone for the whole usable fort, courtyard, and wings.
bannermod.surveyor.tooltip.loop_1: Manual build loop: fort first, then storage, farm, homes, and the specialist workshops.
bannermod.surveyor.tooltip.loop_2: Free citizens become workers when they reach a building anchor with an open vacancy.
bannermod.surveyor.no_session: No survey session. Mark an anchor first.
bannermod.surveyor.marks_reset: All survey marks cleared for this mode.
bannermod.surveyor.guide_preview.enabled: Guide preview enabled for this survey mode.
bannermod.surveyor.guide_preview.disable: Guide preview hidden. Only your anchor and marked zones remain visible.
bannermod.surveyor.mode_hint.bootstrap_f: Build the fort from the hologram, then mark the authority spot and the walkable interior.
bannermod.surveyor.mode_hint.house: Build a small roofed home first, then mark the walkable room and the bed area.
bannermod.surveyor.mode_hint.farm: Build or plant the field first, then mark the full crop and farmland work area.
bannermod.surveyor.mode_hint.mine: Build the mine entrance or shed first, then mark the exposed mine face or tunnel work area.
bannermod.surveyor.mode_hint.lumber_camp: Build the camp first, then mark the nearby tree, log, and sapling work area.
bannermod.surveyor.mode_hint.smithy: Build a roofed smithy first, then mark the interior and the anvil-furnace work area.
bannermod.surveyor.mode_hint.storage: Build the stockpile or warehouse first, then mark the chests, barrels, and supply racks.
bannermod.surveyor.mode_hint.architect_b: Build a roofed workshop first, then mark the interior and the drafting table work area.
bannermod.surveyor.mode_hint.barracks: Build the barracks first, then mark the main room, bunks, and the chest or armory area.
bannermod.surveyor.mode_hint.inspect_exi: Inspect shows data for an already-validated building at the selected anchor.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.action.hide_gu: Imported or already-built shapes can be surveyed with only your anchor and captured zones visible.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.action.show_gu: Show the teaching hologram again for the current survey mode.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.action.guide_p: Inspect mode already shows only the validated anchor state.
bannermod.surveyor.role_hint.authority_p: mark the banner, command post, or ruling spot around the anchor
bannermod.surveyor.role_hint.interior: mark the walkable inside space with solid floor below and air for feet and head
bannermod.surveyor.role_hint.sleeping: mark the beds or bunks that belong to this building
bannermod.surveyor.role_hint.work_zone: mark the crops, mine face, tools, or resource patch the building uses
bannermod.surveyor.role_hint.storage: mark the chests, barrels, or supply racks that belong to this building
bannermod.surveyor.role_purpose.authorit: command spot for the banner, throne, council table, or hall center
bannermod.surveyor.role_purpose.interior: usable inside air volume where workers or residents can stand and move
bannermod.surveyor.role_purpose.sleeping: bed area that counts as this building's sleeping capacity
bannermod.surveyor.role_purpose.work_zon: job area that defines where this building gathers, crafts, mines, or harvests
bannermod.surveyor.role_purpose.fort_per: defensive shell that marks the fort's protected outer edge
bannermod.surveyor.role_purpose.entrance: boundary opening that shows how the building is entered from outside
bannermod.surveyor.role_purpose.storage: stockpile zone the settlement should treat as this building's supplies
bannermod.surveyor.role_purpose.prefab_f: reserved footprint that shows exactly where the planned structure should sit
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.authority: banner, throne, council table, command desk, or another obvious seat of authority
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.interior: walkable room volume with solid floor, headroom, walls, and roof cover overhead
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.bootstrap: one large INTERIOR zone for the whole walkable fort, courtyard, and side or back wings; split fort interior zones are not supported yet
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.sleeping: beds, bunks, and the clear floor space around them
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.work_zone: the job site, machines, resource face, crops, or harvest patch tied to the building
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.fort_peri: palisades, walls, towers, battlements, and the defended edge of the fort
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.entrance: doorway, gate opening, porch, and a clear path through the boundary
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.storage: chests, barrels, shelves, crates, and covered stockpile space
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.prefab_fo: the exact footprint reserved for this planned structure
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.farm.work: farmland, crops, irrigation water, composters, and walking lanes between rows
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.mine.work: mine face, tunnel mouth, stone or ore front, supports, and safe worker standing space
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.lumber_ca: logs, saplings, stump rows, chopping space, and nearby tree regrowth area
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.smithy.wo: anvils, furnaces, smithing stations, quenching space, and the smith's work lane
bannermod.surveyor.role_blocks.architect: crafting table, drafting space, material pallets, and the builder's staging area
bannermod.surveyor.screen.title: Settlement Surveyor
bannermod.surveyor.screen.mode.tooltip: Pick which building type the next captured zones belong to.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.role.tooltip: Pick which zone role the next captured corners will save.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.role.tooltip.i: Inspect mode does not capture new zones.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.role.tooltip.f: This mode uses one required role: %s - %s.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.role.tooltip.c: Cycle between this mode's required roles. Current role: %s.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.capture_hint: Right-click: anchor or corners. Shift-right-click: reopen this board.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.actions.toolti: Open clear and reset actions without cluttering the board.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.action.cancel_: Cancel the first stored corner so you can start this zone again.
No pending Corner A is stored right now.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.action.clear_r: Remove the saved %s zone and mark it again.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.action.reset_a: Clear anchor, pending corner, and every saved zone for this mode.
There are no survey marks to reset.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.action.pin_pre: Store this survey hologram on the client so it stays visible after you put the surveyor away.
Place an anchor first so the pinned hologram has a fixed location.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.action.unpin_p: Remove the pinned client-side survey hologram.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.requirements.n: No required zones in inspect mode.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.checklist.1: 1. Set anchor with a normal right-click.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.checklist.2: 2. Capture two corners for the selected role.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.checklist.3: 3. Validate when required roles turn green.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.validate.toolt: Run the normal server-side survey validation.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.validate.need_: Mark an anchor in the world first.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.next.anchor: Next step: right-click the building's anchor block in the world.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.next.bootstrap: Next step: capture one INTERIOR zone for the whole usable fort, courtyard, and wings.
bannermod.surveyor.screen.next.validate: Next step: press Validate when every checklist item is green.
bannermod.surveyor.hud.title: Starter Fort Survey
bannermod.surveyor.hud.fort_legend: Color key: gold or wood = walls and towers, orange = authority anchor, blue = usable interior or courtyard, brown = wings, storage, or barracks space.
bannermod.surveyor.hud.pinned: Pinned preview active on this client.
bannermod.surveyor.hud.pinned_help: Pinned preview is read-only. Re-equip the surveyor to move zones or validate.
bannermod.surveyor.hud.preview: Preview follows your aim until the anchor is placed.
bannermod.surveyor.hud.next.anchor: Next: right-click the building's anchor block.
bannermod.surveyor.hud.next.authority: Next: mark AUTHORITY_POINT around the anchor.
bannermod.surveyor.hud.next.interior: Next: mark INTERIOR inside the built fort.
bannermod.surveyor.hud.next.capture: Next: capture the required zone outlines for this mode.
bannermod.surveyor.hud.next.bootstrap_in: Next: capture one INTERIOR zone for the whole usable fort, courtyard, and wings.
bannermod.surveyor.hud.next.validate: Next: open the survey board and press Validate.
bannermod.onboarding.founded.next: Settlement founded. Next: add storage, a farm, homes, and later a market.
bannermod.onboarding.founded.staffing: Starter workers keep their mechanics. Free citizens only convert when they reach an open vacancy anchor.
bannermod.onboarding.vacancy: Open vacancy: %s x%s. Watch for a nearby free citizen to walk in and convert.
bannermod.onboarding.next.storage: Next step: add storage so the settlement can see stockpiles and route endpoints.
bannermod.onboarding.next.farm: Next step: add a farm to open the first citizen-run profession loop.
bannermod.onboarding.next.house: Next step: add homes so free citizens have room to stay near the settlement.
bannermod.onboarding.next.professions: Next step: add mine, lumber camp, and architect workshop to wake the starter miner, lumberjack, and builder loops.
bannermod.onboarding.next.market: Next step: add a market so trade and state-promotion infrastructure are complete.
bannermod.onboarding.prefab_hint.storage: Starter priority: storage lets the settlement read stockpiles and routes.
bannermod.onboarding.prefab_hint.farm: Starter priority: this opens the easiest first worker loop for nearby free citizens.
bannermod.onboarding.prefab_hint.market_: Promotion reminder: fort or town hall + storage + market are the minimum political base.
bannermod.onboarding.prefab_hint.house: Population support: homes keep citizen growth from stalling.
bannermod.onboarding.prefab_hint.mine: Profession unlock: this gives the waiting starter miner a real workplace.
bannermod.onboarding.prefab_hint.lumber_: Profession unlock: this gives the waiting starter lumberjack a real workplace.
bannermod.onboarding.prefab_hint.barrack: Military expansion: barracks spawn recruit vacancies instead of civilian jobs.
gui.bannermod.governor.action.disabled.s: Refresh pending after the last order
gui.bannermod.governor.tax_consequence.u: Unpaid: treasury stalls; upkeep/defense can starve.
gui.bannermod.governor.title: Governor: %s
gui.bannermod.claim_overlay.occupied_con: Owner actions locked; occupier controls this claim
chat.bannermod.claim_protection.denied.f: This friendly claim is protected right now; owner actions are locked here.
chat.bannermod.claim_protection.denied.h: Hostile claim protection blocks that action here.
chat.bannermod.claim_protection.denied.u: Wilderness edits are disabled on this server. Claim land before building or breaking here.
chat.bannermod.war_outcome.denied.not_au: War outcome denied: you are not authorized for the attacking state.
chat.bannermod.war_outcome.denied.not_fo: War outcome denied: war declaration not found.
chat.bannermod.war_outcome.denied.op_onl: War outcome denied: %s is reserved for operators.
chat.bannermod.war_outcome.denied.reject: War outcome denied: %s failed server validation (%s).
chat.bannermod.war_outcome.denied.unsupp: War outcome denied: %s is not supported for %s wars.

occupied chunks are controlled and taxed until cleared
gui.bannermod.war_list.consequence.revol: revolt pressure and aftermath visible below
gui.bannermod.war_list.consequence.tribu: tribute victory can debit defender treasury
tribute settled; defender has temporary redeclaration immunity
gui.bannermod.war_list.detail.revolt_ui: Revolt UI: operators may mark the first pending revolt won or failed
gui.bannermod.war_list.outcome_status.an: annex unlocks for attacking leader at claim chunk
gui.bannermod.war_list.outcome_status.oc: occupy unlocks for attacking leader at current chunk
gui.bannermod.war_list.outcome_status.tr: tribute unlocks for op attacking leader
gui.bannermod.war_list.outcome_hint: Outcome buttons unlock for the attacking leader.
gui.bannermod.war_list.revolt.failed_aft: Revolt failed: %s could not break occupation at t%s
gui.bannermod.war_list.revolt.pending_ob: Objective: hold %s during battle window with no defenders
gui.bannermod.war_list.revolt.pending_pr: Pressure: %s rebels against occupation, due t%s
gui.bannermod.war_list.revolt.success_af: Revolt won: %s broke occupation at t%s
gui.bannermod.war_list.revolt.success_re: Aftermath: occupation removed; land can be reclaimed
gui.bannermod.war_list.title: Wars
gui.bannermod.war_list.tooltip.active_le: Select an active war where you lead one side.
gui.bannermod.war_list.tooltip.attacker_: Only the attacking leader can resolve this war.
gui.bannermod.war_list.tooltip.no_declar: You need authority over an active state that can declare offensive wars.
gui.bannermod.war_list.tooltip.op_only: This outcome is reserved for operators.
gui.bannermod.war_list.tooltip.revolt_no: Selected war has no pending revolt to resolve.
gui.bannermod.war_list.tooltip.revolt_se: Resolve the first pending revolt shown in the detail panel.
gui.bannermod.war_list.tooltip.unsupport: Selected war goal does not support this outcome.
gui.bannermod.war_list.tooltip.war_close: Resolved or cancelled wars cannot be changed.
gui.bannermod.war_list.feedback.pending: Outcome request sent; server will accept or deny in chat.
gui.bannermod.war.ally_denial.invitee_al: That state is already on this side.
gui.bannermod.war.ally_denial.invitee_is: Main war sides cannot be invited as allies.
gui.bannermod.war.ally_denial.invitee_on: That state is already on the opposing side.
gui.bannermod.war.ally_denial.peaceful_c: Peaceful states cannot join the attacking side.
gui.bannermod.war.ally_denial.war_not_pr: Allies can only be invited before the war becomes active.
gui.bannermod.war.denial.attacker_status: Attacker status %s cannot declare offensive war.
gui.bannermod.war.denial.co_leader_monar: You are listed as co-leader, but this state is a monarchy; only the leader can act.
gui.bannermod.war.denial.leader_only: Only this state leader (or an operator) can do that.
gui.bannermod.war.denial.no_defender: No other state is available as defender.
gui.bannermod.war.denial.no_state: No political state is selected or synced.
gui.bannermod.war.denial.not_authorized: Only the realm leader, an authorized republic co-leader, or an operator can do that.
gui.bannermod.war.denial.outsider_republ: Only this republic's leader, co-leaders, or an operator can do that.
gui.bannermod.war.siege_denial.duplicate: A siege standard already exists at that position.
gui.bannermod.war.siege_denial.side_not_: Side is not a participant of this war.
gui.bannermod.war.siege_denial.unsupport: Siege standards can only be placed in the Overworld.
gui.bannermod.war.siege_denial.war_close: War is closed; cannot place a siege standard.
gui.bannermod.war_allies.title: War Allies
gui.bannermod.war_declare.hint: Pick sides, goal, and optional casus belli.

chat.bannermod.revolt.denied.occupation_: Revolt denied: the occupation is already gone.
chat.bannermod.revolt.denied.op_only: Revolt denied: only operators can manually resolve revolts.
chat.bannermod.revolt.denied.resolve_fai: Revolt denied: server could not resolve the revolt.
